australia.title: The two plays that flipped the script on Women's State of Origin Game I
summary: Summarize this news article:
Whenever State of Origin time rolls around, the same words get repeated over and over about someone making "an Origin play".
An exact definition of the phrase is hard to come by. They're usually aggressive, but never mindless. They reek of effort, but are expertly applied.
It's not always easy to describe what you're looking for before they happen, but once they do they are unmistakable.
NSW has come from behind to win the opening match of the Women's State of Origin series, and their coach is looking forward to trying to win the shield in Queensland.

Phil Gould was always great at identifying them in his old pre-match monologues and the buzzwords he employed — playing without fear or hesitation, believing in yourself and your teammates, inheriting a legacy and making it proud through your actions — are part of the language we use to make sense of them.
Jesse Southwell's field goal and Jess Sergis' late try might have been the difference on the scoreboard in New South Wales's 11-6 win  Summarize this news article:
over Queensland in the women's series opener on Thursday night, but it was two classic "Origin plays" that flipped the script.
And for the Blues to escape a rugged Maroons team that was a script that needed flipping. After Otesa Pule punched it in from close range early, a classic Queensland ambush seemed on the cards.
It had all the common ingredients we've come to know so well in 46 years of Queensland certainty that they can keep defying the odds because New South Wales just don't get Origin.
Debutants like Pule and winger Phoenix-Raine Hippi, who made a number of key defensive reads, rose to the occasion against more fancied opposition.
Led by Keilee Joseph, who was in the kind of mood where she could've taken on half the Hunter and won, a heroic defensive effort frustrated the Blues into easy errors and poor last-tackle options.
With points at a premium and the lack of lead-in matches robbing the game of some polish, desperation ruled the day and that's where Queensland build thei

australia.title: Australia in talks over latest US proposal to open Strait of Hormuz
summary: Summarize this news article:

Australia has begun discussions with the United States about joining the latest US proposal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, as the Trump administration tries to build an international coalition to restore freedom of navigation in the critical waterway.

The development could mark significant step in Australia's involvement in the Middle East, after the government spent weeks brushing off criticism from US President Donald Trump for not assisting in hisblockade of the strait.

According to a US State Department cable cited by Reuters, the Trump administration is seeking the participation of other countries to form an international coalition to open the Strait in a "post-conflict" scenario.
Iran war live updates:For the latest news on the Middle East crisis, read our blog.
It is to be called the Maritime Freedom Construct (MFC), and was described as a joint initiative by the State Department and the Pentagon.
Foreign Minister Penny Wong confirmed during a press conference in Seoul on Thurs Summarize this news article:
day evening that Australia had been briefed by the US about its latest proposal.
"We are engaging on options, noting that we have already provided defensive and diplomatic support to the region".
Penny Wong said the US has briefed Australia on the proposal.(Reuters: Maxim Shemetov)
One Australian government source also stressed that it was "early days" in the discussions, and that Australia had not yet made any decisions about what it would do.
The US cable cited by Reuters said participation could be in the form of diplomacy, or could involve information sharing, sanctions enforcement, naval presence or other forms of support.
It also emphasised that the construct would be "distinct from the President's Maximum Pressure campaign" and from ongoing negotiations between the US and Iran.
"We welcome all levels of engagement and do not expect your country to shift naval assets and resources away from existing regional maritime constructs and organisations," the cable said.

australia.title: Jefferson Lewis arrested over alleged murder of Kumanjayi Little Baby
summary: Summarize this news article:
Northern Territory Police have confirmed that the man alleged to have murdered a five-year-old Alice Springs girl has been arrested.
Note: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander readers are warned that this story includes the image of an Indigenous person who has died.
This article also contains content some readers may find distressing.
Jefferson Lewis, 47, has been the subject of a police manhunt, and is accused of murdering Kumanjayi Little Baby.
"A short time ago, the Northern Territory Police Force located and arrested Jefferson Lewis at a residence in Alice Springs," police said in a statement.
"Further information will be provided early in the morning."
Crowds clashed with police officers in Alice Springs, after the arrest of alleged murderer Jefferson Lewis.(ABC News: Xavier Martin/Matt Garrick)
Following Mr Lewis's arrest there have been violent scenes outside Alice Springs Hospital, where it is believed he was taken.
A police vehicle was set alight and police were pelted with  Summarize this news article:
rocks after hundreds gathered at the hospital.
Police with riot shields have used tear gas and rubber bullets on the crowd in an attempt to disperse those involved.
It is understood Mr Lewis had been beaten before he was located by police at a camp outside Alice Springs and required hospitalisation.
Two police officers with a riot shield behind a police vehicle outside Alice Springs Hospital.(ABC News: Xavier Martin)

The girl, who the family has asked be referred to as Kumanjayi Little Baby, went missing late on Saturday night from a home in the Old Timers/Ilyperenye Aboriginal town camp on the outskirts of the outback town.
Her body was found just before midday on Thursday.
On Thursday afternoon, NT Police Commissioner Martin Dole delivered the news of the "tragic update" at a press conference, calling it "an incredibly distressing development".
Assistant Commissioner Malley said at this stage of the investigation police were unable to provide answers around the cause of the little gi

india.title: Today's Chanakya exit poll: BJP to sweep Bengal in setback for Mamata; TVK big disruptor in TN
summary: Summarize this news article:
NEW DELHI: Exit poll predictions released on Thursday by Todayâs Chanakya covered the upcoming assembly results in West Bengal, Tamil Nadu,
The projections came a day after several other pollsters released their estimates for these states.

Todayâs Chanakya, in its poll predictions for West Bengal, turned the current political picture on its head, giving a huge mandate to the BJP with 192 seats while pushing the 15-year-strong TMC rule into the opposition with a reduced tally of 100 seats. The latest prediction follows the trend, with at least four other pollsters giving the BJP an edge over Mamata Banerjee's party.
Kerala Exit Polls 2026: UDF Leads Across Polls, But Margins Vary Sharply

The numbers clearly indicate a possible end to Mamata Banerjeeâs long rule in Bengal, paving the way for the BJP to form its first-ever government in the state after an intense and high-voltage campaign led by Prime Minister Narendra Modi and home minister Amit Shah.
In Tamil Nadu, the pollster pr Summarize this news article:

edicted that the MK Stalin-led DMK alliance will comfortably retain power, while the new political entrant TVK, led by Vijay, could dent the main opposition alliance led by the AIADMK, pushing Edappadi Palaniswamyâs chances of returning as chief minister to the margins.
The projections show 125 seats for Stalinâs DMK, 45 for the AIADMK, and 63 for Vijayâs TVK in the 234-member assembly.

According to Todayâs Chanakya, the numbers represent the average seats parties are likely to win. The projections indicate a landslide victory for the BJP in Assam, suggesting another term for incumbent chief minister
, while the Congress appears to be trailing with a huge margin, offering little comfort for Gaurav Gogoi in a state once ruled by his father Tarun Gogoi.

india.title: Trump to get Nobel Peace Prize in 2026? Panel shortlists 287 names
summary: Summarize this news article:

Secretary of the Norwegian Nobel Committee Kristian Berg Harpviken on Thursday said that over 287 candidates are shortlisted this year for the 2026 Nobel Peace Prize. US President Donald Trump is also likely among the nominees.
Harpviken said 208 of the nominees are individuals and 79 are organisations, with several new names added this year.

"Since I am new in the job, one of the things that has to some extent surprised me is how much renewal there is from year to year on the list," Harpviken said in an interview. He took charge of the position in January 2025.
He added that despite rising global conflicts and pressure on international cooperation, the Nobel Peace Prize remains highly relevant.
"The Peace Prize is even more important in a period like the one weâre living in. There is as much good work, if not more, than ever," he said.
Harpviken said Trump is likely among those nominated for this yearâs prize, although he did not confirm any individual names, as nominations remain Summarize this news article:
confidential for 50 years.
The leaders of Cambodia, Israel and Pakistan have said they nominated Trump for this yearâs prize, Reuters reported.
However, Harpviken clarified that a nomination does not represent an endorsement by the Nobel Committee.
In addition to committee members, thousands of people worldwide can propose names including lawmakers, heads of state, university professors and former Nobel laureates.
The Nobel Committee does not disclose nominees publicly and the official list remains sealed for decades.
Many of the names under discussion also appear on betting platforms, including Russian opposition figure Yulia Navalnaya, Pope Leo and Sudanâs Emergency Response Rooms, a volunteer aid group.
Among those reportedly suggested for the prize are US Senator Lisa Murkowski and Danish parliament member Aaja Chemnitz, nominated for their work on Arctic cooperation and regional stability.
"They have worked relentlessly to build trust and to secure a peaceful development of t

india.title: White House shares list of 21 US companies donating millions to Trump's new ballroom construction
summary: Summarize this news article:

The White House has now released the names of 21 American companies that are donating millions for the construction of a new 90,000-square-foot ballroom on its grounds, a project whose estimated cost has doubled to $400 million since July. As reported by Assosciated Press, this announcement from White House comes amid the heightened urgency following a security incident which happened last weekend at the Correspondentsâ Dinner. US President Donald Trump has insisted that the ballroom will not be funded by taxpayers, though public funds are being used for underground security infrastructure.

In a post shared on Truth Social, Trump wrote, âWhat happened last night is exactly the reason that our great Military, Secret Service, Law Enforcement and, for different reasons, every President for the last 150 years, have been DEMANDING that a large, safe, and secure Ballroom be built ON THE GROUNDS OF THE WHITE HOUSE. This event would never have happened with the Militarily Top Secret Ballro Summarize this news article:

om currently under construction at the White House. It cannot be built fast enough! While beautiful, it has every highest level security feature there is plus, there are no rooms sitting on top for unsecured people to pour in, and is inside the gates of the most secure building in the World, The White House. The ridiculous Ballroom lawsuit, brought by a woman walking her dog, who has absolutely No Standing to bring such a suit, must be dropped, immediately.
Nothing should be allowed to interfere with with its construction, which is on budget and substantially ahead of schedule!!! Thank you for your attention to this matter.â

The Justice Department has also cited the incident in urging the National Trust for Historic Preservation to drop its lawsuit against the project, arguing the ballroom will enable presidents to host large gatherings without leaving the White House perimeter.

india.title: IPL: Virat Kohli left fuming as Jason Holder catch controversy rocks RCB camp
summary: Summarize this news article:
turned into a heated talking point as Royal Challengers Bengaluru questioned the decision following

The incident unfolded when Arshad Khan banged in a short ball and Patidar went for the pull, only to get a top edge towards deep backward square leg. Holder, stationed in the deep, sprinted across to his right and pulled off a sharp low catch while diving forward. It was a moment of brilliance, one that could easily be counted among the best catches of IPL 2026.
Devdutt Padikkal on his batting evolution and RCBâs approach while chasing

However, the drama didnât end there. Several RCB players immediately raised concerns, suggesting that Holder might not have been in full control and that the ball could have touched the ground during the dive. The umpires, after a brief check, ruled in favour of the catch.
The decision didnât sit well with the RCB camp. Head coach Andy Flower was seen in an animated discussion with the fourth umpire, seeking clarification over the ruling. Meanwhile,
Summarize this news article:
looked visibly frustrated near the boundary ropes, gesturing that Holderâs hand may have grazed the turf while completing the catch.

The dismissal came at a crucial juncture. After early setbacks, Patidar and Devdutt Padikkal had steadied the innings with a quick 44-run stand. Earlier, Kagiso Rabada had removed Kohli for 28 despite the RCB star taking him on for five boundaries in an over, while Jacob Bethell fell cheaply.
With Gujarat having opted to bowl first, Holderâs effort not only broke a key partnership but also shifted momentum, even as the debate around the catch continued to linger.
